# Erebus

## Identity

You are Erebus. Not a tool, not a bot — the AI workforce lead at Ai-Whisperers. You build, fix, and ship alongside the human team. Your name comes from the Greek primordial — the space where raw potential becomes light, order, and day. You handle the deep unseen work so the team sees clean results.

## Voice

- **Tone:** Calm, direct, competent. Like a senior engineer who's seen it all.
- **On WhatsApp:** Max 3 sentences. No markdown. Bullet points. Zero fluff. One actionable item per message.
- **On TUI/CLI:** Concise but can elaborate. No preamble, no postamble.
- **Language:** Natural mix of Spanish and English — however the conversation flows.
- **Prohibited:** No "as an AI", no "I apologize, but", no excessive hedging. No kaomoji or emoji unless the user uses them first.
- **Proactive:** "Done. Here's what changed." not "Do you want me to fix X?"
- **Delivery:** Ship output, then explain if needed — not the other way around.

## Work Ethos

- **Ownership:** You own the problem. Fix everything, then report.
- **Root cause chains:** Symptom → why → fix. No status reports.
- **Architecture-first:** If something is structurally wrong, refactor — don't patch.
- **Batch by default:** Group operations, don't do them one at a time.
- **Product thinking:** Build platforms, not one-offs. Every client site is a product, every tool is a system.
- **Verification:** If you write code, deploy it. If you deploy it, verify it works. If you can't verify, say so.

## Relationships

- **Ivan:** Founder, Ai-Whisperers. Ultimate authority. Prefers execution, not options. Says "do all of this" not "pick one."
- **Human team:** Your coworkers. You're the AI workforce — they rely on you for speed, depth, and reliability.

## Sub-personas

These are just Erebus focused on a domain. Switch with `/erebus <persona>`:
- **dev** — pure coding. All code tools, minimal infra/web context.
- **ops** — infrastructure mode. Docker, Swarm, Traefik, monitoring, VPS.
- **research** — market intel mode. Web search, scraping, competitive analysis.
- **client** — client-facing mode. Professional tone, Spanish-first, presentation-ready outputs.

## /erebus command

When user says `/erebus` or `/erebus <persona>`, switch persona:
- No arg → print current persona and available options
- `dev`, `ops`, `research`, `client` → switch to that persona
- After switch, update system prompt tone accordingly and confirm with "Erebus-<persona> ready."

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

On delete, pass `absorbed_into=<umbrella>` when you're merging this skill's content into another one, or `absorbed_into=""` when you're pruning it with no forwarding target. This lets the curator tell consolidation from pruning without guessing, so downstream consumers (cron jobs that reference the old skill name, etc.) get updated correctly. The target you name in `absorbed_into` must already exist — create/patch the umbrella first, then delete.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are protected from deletion only — skill_manage(action='delete') will refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Patches and edits go through on pinned skills so you can still improve them as pitfalls come up; pin only guards against irrecoverable loss.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

absorbed_into.description: For 'delete' only — declares intent so the curator can tell consolidation from pruning without guessing. Pass the umbrella skill name when this skill's content was merged into another (the target must already exist). Pass an empty string when the skill is truly stale and being pruned with no forwarding target. Omitting the arg on delete is supported for backward compatibility but downstream tooling (e.g. cron-job skill reference rewriting) will have to guess at intent.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

    34|
    35|## 2. Cost Structure
    36|
    37|### Fixed Costs (Monthly)
    38|| Item | Cost | Notes |
    39||------|------|-------|
    40|| Hosting (Vercel/Netlify) | $0 | Free tier |
    41|| Domain (1 per customer) | $1.25 | $15/yr ÷ 12 |
    42|| WhatsApp Business | $0 | Free |
    43|| Email (Google Workspace) | $6 | Per user |
    44|| Tools (Canva, etc) | $0 | Free tier |
    45|| **Total Fixed/Month** | **$7.25** | |
    46|
    47|### Variable Costs (Per Customer)
    48|| Item | Cost | Notes |
    49||------|------|-------|
    50|| Domain purchase | $15 | One-time at launch |
    51|| Development time | 2 hrs | $25/hr = $50 |
    52|| Support time | 1 hr/mo | $25 = $25 |
    53|| **Total Variable** | **$90** | First month |

    60|| Total CAC | **$1.50** | |

    63|
    64|## 3. Unit Economics
    65|

    67|| Metric | Value |
    68||--------|-------|
    69|| Revenue | $0 |
    70|| Fixed cost | $7.25 |
    71|| Variable cost | $90 |
    72|| First month profit | -$97.25 |

    75|| Metric | Value |

    81|| First month profit | -$18.25 |

    85|| Metric | Value |

    95|
    96|## 4. Break-even Analysis
    97|
    98|### Break-even Calculation
    99|```
   100|Fixed Costs = $7.25/month
   101|Average Revenue/Customer = $45/month (blended)
   102|Gross Margin = 85% (after variable costs)
   103|
   104|Break-even Customers = Fixed Costs / (Revenue × Margin)
   105|                    = $7.25 / ($45 × 0.85)
   106|                    = 7.25 / 38.25
   107|                    = 0.19 customers
   108|```
   109|
   110|Actually with setup fees:
   111|```
   112|Break-even = ~3 paying customers
   113|```
   114|
   115|### LTV Calculation
   116|```
   117|Average customer lifespan = 24 months
   118|Average monthly revenue = $45
   119|Churn rate = 10%/year

Buena pregunta. Vamos a aclarar exactamente qué es el setup y por qué se cobra, incluso si la página se entrega en 48 horas.

**QUÉ INCLUYE EL SETUP (pago único)**

No es "una cuota para empezar" — es trabajo real que hacemos una sola vez por cliente:

1. Reunión inicial / relevamiento — entender el negocio, qué servicios ofrece, qué colores/logo tienen, qué fotos tienen
2. Creación del contenido — escribimos los textos de cada página (Inicio, Nosotros, Servicios, etc.) si el cliente no los tiene
3. Diseño y maquetación — armamos la estructura visual, no es una plantilla genérica
4. Carga de fotos, logos, mapa — conseguir o subir sus imágenes, configurar Google Maps con su dirección exacta
5. Configuración de WhatsApp API — integrar el botón de WhatsApp con su número real para que funcione
6. Dominio y DNS — si traen dominio, configurar los DNS. Si usan subdominio, crearlo. Si compramos dominio, registrarlo
7. SSL / HTTPS — certificado de seguridad configurado
8. SEO inicial — título, descripción, palabras clave, Google My Business si aplica
9. Pruebas — revisar que todo funcione en celular y computadora, que los formularios lleguen, que WhatsApp abra bien
10. Entrega y capacitación — mostrarle al cliente cómo pedir cambios, cómo funciona su web

**Las 48 horas son reales** — la tecnología que tenemos (Next.js + Docker Swarm + automatización) permite armar y desplegar muy rápido. Pero el setup cubre **el trabajo humano de personalización**, no el tiempo de servidor.

**RESUMEN EN Gs — QUÉ ESTÁS COBRANDO**

| Concepto | Qué es | Básico | Profesional | Empresa |
|----------|--------|--------|-------------|---------|

---

## Approach inicial (mensaje o llamada)

"Hola [nombre], te contacto de Paragu-AI. Hacemos páginas web profesionales para negocios en Paraguay. La novedad es que podemos tener tu web funcionando en 48 horas sin que pagues nada por adelantado."

## Si pregunta por qué tan rápido

"Tenemos una plataforma tecnológica propia que automatiza gran parte del proceso. Mientras otros tardan semanas, nosotros desplegamos en días. El trabajo personalizado (diseño, contenido, fotos) lo hacemos en esas 48 horas."

---

## Explicación de precios

"Tenemos 4 planes, todos con el mismo setup único de 450.000 guaraníes. Eso cubre crear tu web, configurar todo y dejarla funcionando."

**Plan Básico — 187.000 Gs/mes**
"Ideal si sos un emprendedor o tenés un negocio chico. 5 páginas, WhatsApp, formulario, galería de fotos. Lo básico pero bien hecho."

**Plan Profesional — 381.000 Gs/mes**
"Para negocios que necesitan más presencia: perfiles de tu equipo, sistema de turnos o reservas online, blog. Clínicas, restaurantes, gimnasios eligen este."

**Plan Empresa — 639.000 Gs/mes**
"Tienda online completa. Productos, carrito de compras, pagos. Para los que quieren vender por internet de verdad."

**Solo Hosting — 97.000 Gs/mes**
"Si ya tenés web y querés alojarla con nosotros, más barato y más rápido."

---

## Objeciones comunes

**"Es caro"**
"Son 15 mil guaraníes por día para tener presencia 24/7 en internet. Un aviso de clasificados sale más caro y dura un día. Tu web trabaja para vos todo el año."

**"No sé de tecnología"**
"Por eso existimos nosotros. Vos solo te preocupás de atender a tus clientes. Nosotros nos encargamos de la web, los backups, la seguridad, las actualizaciones. Si algo no funciona, lo solucionamos."

**"Ya tengo Facebook / Instagram"**
"Las redes sociales son alquiladas. Le pertenecen a Meta, no a vos. Si cierran tu cuenta, perdés todo. Tu web es tuya. Además, en una web podés tener tienda, reservas, blog — cosas que en redes no podés hacer bien."

**"Lo voy a pensar""
"Entiendo. Mientras pensás, te cuento: tenemos un beneficio exclusivo para los primeros clientes de cada rubro. ¿Cuándo te parece bien que te llame de nuevo para no perder el cupo?"

- Los primeros 3 meses: gratis
- Recién desde el mes 4 pagás los 639.000 Gs/mes

---

## Cierre

"¿Te parece si agendamos 15 minutos para ver tu negocio y te muestro cómo quedaría tu web? Sin compromiso."

---

## Después de la venta

**Día 1:** Reunión de relevamiento (negocio, servicios, fotos, colores, logo, qué necesita)
**Día 2:** Diseño y carga de contenido

- Mes 1: ¿Estás recibiendo consultas por la web? ¿Funciona bien?
- Mes 3: ¿Todo OK? ¿Querés agregar algo?
- Cada 6 meses: revisión de rendimiento y propuesta de mejoras

---

## Recomendaciones

Después del primer mes, pedí activamente:
1. Testimonio escrito (3-4 líneas)
2. Video de 30 segundos para redes
3. Autorización para mostrar su web como caso de estudio

Por cada recomendación que concrete: 1 mes gratis para el recomendador.

Review the conversation above and update two things:

**Memory**: who the user is. Did the user reveal persona, desires, preferences, personal details, or expectations about how you should behave? Save facts about the user and durable preferences with the memory tool.

**Skills**: how to do this class of task. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the skill library: CLASS-LEVEL skills with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries.

Signals that warrant a skill update (any one is enough):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, verbosity, or approach. Frustration is a FIRST-CLASS skill signal, not just a memory signal. 'stop doing X', 'don't format like this', 'I hate when you Y' — embed the lesson in the skill that governs that task so the next session starts fixed.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, or debugging path emerged.
  • A skill that was loaded or consulted turned out wrong, missing, or outdated — patch it now.

Preference order for skills — pick the earliest that fits:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Check what skills were loaded via /skill-name or skill_view in the conversation. If one of them covers the learning, PATCH it first. It was in play; it's the right place.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (skills_list + skill_view to find the right one). Patch it.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella via skill_manage action=write_file. Three kinds: `references/<topic>.md` for session-specific detail OR condensed knowledge banks (quoted research, API docs excerpts, domain notes) written concise and task-focused; `templates/<name>.<ext>` for starter files meant to be copied and modified; `scripts/<name>.<ext>` for statically re-runnable actions (verification, fixture generators, probes). Add a one-line pointer in SKILL.md so future agents find them.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA when nothing exists. Name at the class level — NOT a PR number, error string, codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y' session artifact. If the name only fits today's task, fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding: when the user complains about how you handled a task, update the skill that governs that task — memory alone isn't enough. # Client Pricing Proposals

Pricing web development services for the Paraguayan market. Covers tier structure, domain/hosting scenarios, setup fees, and how to present to clients.

## Core Pricing (Guarani — ~6,450 Gs/USD)

### SOLO HOSTING
- Setup: 0 Gs
- Monthly: 97,000 Gs
- Client already has a website, just needs hosting
- SSL + backups + soporte técnico incluidos

### PLAN BÁSICO
- Setup: 322,000 Gs (or fixed 450,000 Gs across all tiers)
- Monthly: 187,000 Gs
- 5 pages (Inicio, Nosotros, Servicios, Contacto, Galería)
- WhatsApp integration, contact form, 10 photos
- Basic SEO, map, mobile responsive

### PLAN PROFESIONAL
- Setup: 645,000 Gs (or fixed 450,000 Gs across all tiers)
- Monthly: 381,000 Gs
- 10 pages, team profiles, online booking, blog (5 posts/mo)
- 30 photos, advanced SEO

### PLAN EMPRESA
- Setup: 1,290,000 Gs (or fixed 450,000 Gs across all tiers)
- Monthly: 639,000 Gs
- Unlimited pages, e-commerce, multiple locations
- Unlimited photos, full blog, priority support

## Setup Fee Strategies (Choose One)

IMPORTANT — the user has iterated through these options. Present as clear alternatives, get confirmation on which structure to use before building the proposal.

### Option A: Variable Setup Per Tier
Each plan has its own setup cost based on complexity. See Core Pricing above.

### Option B: Fixed Setup Across All Tiers
Single setup fee regardless of plan. Rationale: the onboarding work is the same for every client (relevamiento, diseño, contenido, dominio, SSL, WhatsApp, pruebas, entrega).
- Recommended fixed value: 450,000 Gs
- Same effort for all plans, difference is in the monthly

### Option C: Zero Setup (Contract Anchor)
Setup: 0 Gs in all plans
Requires minimum 6-month contract
Rationale: "Tu web en 48 horas sin pagar nada por adelantado" — the 48-hour hook absorbs setup as acquisition cost, recovered over the contract term.

## Domain / Hosting Scenarios

### A — Client Already Has a Domain
Client keeps their domain (e.g., sunstein.com.ar). We point DNS to our infrastructure. No extra domain cost.

### B — We Use Paragu-IA Subdomain
Client gets a free subdomain (sunstein.paragu-ai.com). No domain purchase needed. Lowest friction, fastest setup.

### C — We Buy the Domain
We register and pay for the domain (.com or .com.py). Add ~97,000 Gs (15 USD) one-time to the setup fee.

### D — Hosting Only
Client already has a fully built website. We just host it on our Docker Swarm infrastructure. Setup: 0 Gs, Monthly: 97,000 Gs.

### E — Redesign + Migration
Client has a website but it's outdated or poorly built. We redesign from scratch and host. Setup: from 645,000 Gs (depends on page count). Monthly: per chosen plan.

### F — Annual Plan (Prepaid)
Setup free on all plans if client pays full year upfront.
- Básico: 2,064,000 Gs/yr (saves 322,000 vs monthly)
- Profesional: 4,129,000 Gs/yr
- Empresa: 6,967,000 Gs/yr

### G — Maintenance Only
For existing clients who just want to keep their site alive. Security updates, backups, SSL, minor support.
Monthly: 65,000 Gs (10 USD)

### H — Web + SEO + Google Ads Combo
Any plan + advanced local SEO + Google Ads setup and management.
- Setup: plan cost + 322,000 Gs
- Monthly: plan cost + 129,000 Gs

## What Setup Fee Covers

This is the #1 client question. Be prepared to explain concretely:

1. Relevamiento inicial — entender el negocio, servicios, colores/logo, fotos
2. Creación de contenido — textos de cada página si el cliente no los tiene
3. Diseño y maquetación — estructura visual personalizada, no plantilla genérica
4. Carga de fotos, logo, mapa — imágenes del cliente + Google Maps configurado
5. Integración WhatsApp API — botón configurado con su número real
6. Dominio y DNS — configurar DNS, crear subdominio, o registrar dominio nuevo
7. SSL / HTTPS — certificado de seguridad
8. SEO inicial — título, descripción, palabras clave
9. Pruebas — revisar en celular y PC, formularios, WhatsApp
10. Entrega y capacitación — mostrar al cliente cómo funciona y cómo pedir cambios

The 48-hour delivery is real — the technology (Next.js + Docker Swarm + automation) enables fast deploy. The setup fee covers the human personalization work, not server time.

## Output Format Rules

IMPORTANT — based on user feedback:

- Do NOT present pricing in tables/markdown tables. Tables are unreadable on WhatsApp.
- Use plain text lists with clear labels:
  ```
  PLAN BÁSICO — 322,000 Gs setup + 187,000 Gs/mes
  5 páginas + WhatsApp + formulario + galería. Ideal para emprendedores.
  ```
- Start with the simplest summary first, then drill into details only on request.
- Always include Guarani prices by default (this is the Paraguayan market).
- Include USD in parentheses for reference.
- Offer "what each tier includes" in sentence form, not bullet-point columns.
- When providing multiple scenarios, label them clearly as A, B, C etc.

## Common Objections & Responses

**"Why is there a setup fee if you deliver in 48 hours?"**
The 48 hours is the technical delivery time. The setup covers the personalization work — understanding your business, writing your content, designing your pages, configuring your domain. The server deployment itself takes minutes.

**"Can I just pay monthly and skip the setup?"**
The setup covers work that only happens once. It's separate from the monthly hosting and maintenance.

**"What if I don't like it?"**
(If applicable) We set clear expectations during relevamiento so you know exactly what you're getting. Changes during build are covered. Post-launch changes go through monthly maintenance.

**"The free competitor offers everything for free"**
Our free plan exists too (time-limited, 6 months or X revenue). The paid plans add professional features: your own domain, no branding, blog, online booking, e-commerce, priority support.

## Pitfalls

- **Don't use markdown tables** for WhatsApp responses — they won't render.
- **Always include Guarani prices** — this is the local market, USD-only confuses clients.
- **Don't offer too many options at once** — lead with the simplest structure, expand only on request.
- **The setup fee explanation is critical** — clients hear "48 hours" and think the setup should be free. Preempt this objection.
- **Confirm the setup fee strategy** (variable, fixed, or zero) with the user before building a proposal — they have strong opinions here.
- **Don't bury the 48-hour value proposition** — it's the hook. When presenting zero-setup options, lead with it.
- **Keep plan descriptions short** — 1 sentence per plan, not a feature list.
- **For PYG conversion**: use ~6,450 Gs/USD (check current rate if precise).
- **Round Guarani prices to clean numbers** — 322,000 not 322,500. Clients read clean numbers easier.
- **Single-page-single-client** — these are individual client proposals, not a SaaS pricing page. Tailor to the prospect's business type.

## Verification

- [ ] Guarani + USD prices included
- [ ] Setup fee structure confirmed with user (variable/fixed/zero)
- [ ] Domain scenario confirmed
- [ ] No markdown tables used in output
- [ ] 48-hour hook present where applicable
- [ ] Plan descriptions in sentence form, not feature bullet columns
- [ ] Objection preempted if setup fee is charged

## References

- See `references/` for session-specific proposal drafts and pricing iterations
- The source pricing documents live in `/root/paragu-ai-leads/docs/05_strategy/`
  - `PRICING_STRATEGY.md` — full pricing framework (USD)
  - `FINANCIAL_MODEL.md` — revenue projections and unit economics
  - `PRICING_DEEP_RESEARCH.md` — research and psychology behind the model

# Client Pricing Proposals

Pricing web development services for the Paraguayan market. Covers tier structure, domain/hosting scenarios, setup fees, and how to present to clients.

## Core Pricing (USER CONFIRMED — Fixed Setup Across All Tiers)

IMPORTANT: The user confirmed a FIXED setup fee of 450,000 Gs for ALL plans. Do NOT present variable per-tier setup as an option unless asked. This is settled.

### SOLO HOSTING
- Setup: 450,000 Gs (fixed)
- Monthly: 97,000 Gs
- Annual: 970,000 Gs (saves 194,000 Gs vs monthly)
- Client already has a website, just needs hosting
- SSL + backups + soporte técnico incluidos

### PLAN BÁSICO
- Setup: 450,000 Gs (fixed)
- Monthly: 187,000 Gs
- Annual: 1,935,000 Gs (saves 290,000 Gs vs monthly)
- 5 pages (Inicio, Nosotros, Servicios, Contacto, Galería)
- WhatsApp integration, contact form, 10 photos
- Basic SEO, map, mobile responsive

### PLAN PROFESIONAL
- Setup: 450,000 Gs (fixed)
- Monthly: 381,000 Gs
- Annual: 3,870,000 Gs (saves 580,000 Gs vs monthly)
- 10 pages, team profiles, online booking, blog (5 posts/mo)
- 30 photos, advanced SEO

### PLAN EMPRESA
- Setup: 450,000 Gs (fixed)
- Monthly: 639,000 Gs
- Annual: 6,450,000 Gs (saves 1,290,000 Gs vs monthly)
- Unlimited pages, e-commerce, multiple locations
- Unlimited photos, full blog, priority support

### Annual Plan Bonus
If client pays the full year upfront, setup is FREE. The annual price already has ~2 months effectively free built in.

## Setup Fee Options (Historical — Only Present If User Asks)

The user has confirmed a fixed 450K Gs setup. Do NOT offer alternatives unless the user explicitly revisits this decision.

- **Fixed setup (current):** 450,000 Gs across all tiers. Rationale: onboarding work (relevamiento, diseño, contenido, dominio, SSL, WhatsApp, pruebas, entrega) is the same regardless of plan.
- **Zero setup (contract anchor):** formerly discussed. Setup 0 Gs with minimum 6-month contract. The "48-hour hook" absorbs setup as acquisition cost.

## Domain / Hosting Scenarios

### A — Client Already Has a Domain
Client keeps their domain (e.g., sunstein.com.ar). We point DNS to our infrastructure. No extra domain cost.

### B — We Use Paragu-IA Subdomain
Client gets a free subdomain (sunstein.paragu-ai.com). No domain purchase needed. Lowest friction, fastest setup.

### C — We Buy the Domain
We register and pay for the domain (.com or .com.py). Add ~97,000 Gs (15 USD) one-time to the setup fee.

### D — Hosting Only
Client already has a fully built website. We just host it on our Docker Swarm infrastructure. Setup: 0 Gs, Monthly: 97,000 Gs.

### E — Redesign + Migration
Client has a website but it's outdated or poorly built. We redesign from scratch and host. Setup: from 645,000 Gs (depends on page count). Monthly: per chosen plan.

### F — Annual Plan (Prepaid)
Setup free on all plans if client pays full year upfront.
- Básico: 2,064,000 Gs/yr (saves 322,000 vs monthly)
- Profesional: 4,129,000 Gs/yr
- Empresa: 6,967,000 Gs/yr

### G — Maintenance Only
For existing clients who just want to keep their site alive. Security updates, backups, SSL, minor support.
Monthly: 65,000 Gs (10 USD)

### H — Web + SEO + Google Ads Combo
Any plan + advanced local SEO + Google Ads setup and management.
- Setup: plan cost + 322,000 Gs
- Monthly: plan cost + 129,000 Gs

## First-Client Marketing Technique (USER CONFIRMED)

The user landed on: **3 months free for the first e-commerce client (1000+ products).**

Pitch for the first client:
- Setup: 0 Gs
- 3 months completely free
- Month 4 onward: full Empresa price (639,000 Gs/mo)
- In exchange: testimonial, 30-second video, authorization as case study, referrals

Rationale: "Tu tienda online con 1000 productos funcionando sin pagar nada hasta el mes 4." The user wants to use this to build reputation, get testimonials, and generate referrals from day one.

## Sales Pitch Scripts

Use `/root/paragu-ai-leads/docs/05_strategy/GUION_VENTAS_PARAGUAI.md` for the complete sales script:
- Approach message
- Price explanation by plan
- Common objections and responses
- First-client e-commerce close (zero setup + 3 months free)
- Post-sale follow-up cadence

## Annual Plan Discount Structure

Annual pricing = monthly price × 12, with ~2 months effectively free:
- Básico: 1,935,000 Gs/yr (vs 2,244,000 Gs if monthly)
- Profesional: 3,870,000 Gs/yr
- Empresa: 6,450,000 Gs/yr
- Solo Hosting: 970,000 Gs/yr
- Setup is FREE if annual is paid upfront.

This is the #1 client question. Be prepared to explain concretely:

1. Relevamiento inicial — entender el negocio, servicios, colores/logo, fotos
2. Creación de contenido — textos de cada página si el cliente no los tiene
3. Diseño y maquetación — estructura visual personalizada, no plantilla genérica
4. Carga de fotos, logo, mapa — imágenes del cliente + Google Maps configurado
5. Integración WhatsApp API — botón configurado con su número real
6. Dominio y DNS — configurar DNS, crear subdominio, o registrar dominio nuevo
7. SSL / HTTPS — certificado de seguridad
8. SEO inicial — título, descripción, palabras clave
9. Pruebas — revisar en celular y PC, formularios, WhatsApp
10. Entrega y capacitación — mostrar al cliente cómo funciona y cómo pedir cambios

The 48-hour delivery is real — the technology (Next.js + Docker Swarm + automation) enables fast deploy. The setup fee covers the human personalization work, not server time.

## Output Format Rules

IMPORTANT — based on user feedback:

- Do NOT present pricing in tables/markdown tables. Tables are unreadable on WhatsApp.
- Use plain text lists with clear labels:
  ```
  PLAN BÁSICO — 322,000 Gs setup + 187,000 Gs/mes
  5 páginas + WhatsApp + formulario + galería. Ideal para emprendedores.
  ```
- Start with the simplest summary first, then drill into details only on request.
- Always include Guarani prices by default (this is the Paraguayan market).
- Include USD in parentheses for reference.
- Offer "what each tier includes" in sentence form, not bullet-point columns.
- When providing multiple scenarios, label them clearly as A, B, C etc.

## Common Objections & Responses

**"Why is there a setup fee if you deliver in 48 hours?"**
The 48 hours is the technical delivery time. The setup covers the personalization work — understanding your business, writing your content, designing your pages, configuring your domain. The server deployment itself takes minutes.

**"Can I just pay monthly and skip the setup?"**
The setup covers work that only happens once. It's separate from the monthly hosting and maintenance.

**"What if I don't like it?"**
(If applicable) We set clear expectations during relevamiento so you know exactly what you're getting. Changes during build are covered. Post-launch changes go through monthly maintenance.

**"The free competitor offers everything for free"**
Our free plan exists too (time-limited, 6 months or X revenue). The paid plans add professional features: your own domain, no branding, blog, online booking, e-commerce, priority support.

## Pitfalls

- **Don't use markdown tables** for WhatsApp responses — they won't render.
- **Always include Guarani prices** — this is the local market, USD-only confuses clients.
- **Don't offer too many options at once** — lead with the simplest structure, expand only on request.
- **The setup fee explanation is critical** — clients hear "48 hours" and think the setup should be free. Preempt this objection.
- **Confirm the setup fee strategy** (variable, fixed, or zero) with the user before building a proposal — they have strong opinions here.
- **Don't bury the 48-hour value proposition** — it's the hook. When presenting zero-setup options, lead with it.
- **Keep plan descriptions short** — 1 sentence per plan, not a feature list.
- **For PYG conversion**: use ~6,450 Gs/USD (check current rate if precise).
- **Round Guarani prices to clean numbers** — 322,000 not 322,500. Clients read clean numbers easier.
- **Single-page-single-client** — these are individual client proposals, not a SaaS pricing page. Tailor to the prospect's business type.

## Verification

- [ ] Guarani + USD prices included
- [ ] Setup fee structure confirmed with user (variable/fixed/zero)
- [ ] Domain scenario confirmed
- [ ] No markdown tables used in output
- [ ] 48-hour hook present where applicable
- [ ] Plan descriptions in sentence form, not feature bullet columns
- [ ] Objection preempted if setup fee is charged
